ChatIntent.description: All recognized user intents, mapped to downstream flows.

ChatMessageRequest.description: Body for POST /chat/{conversation_id} — send a new user message in an existing conversation.

allow_crm_write.description: If true, server may propose CRM sync actions, but must still require explicit confirmation.

history.description: Optional recent chat history for stateless clients; server may ignore if it stores history.

analysis_card.description: Optional analysis summary card for FE to render above suggestions.

context.description: Context analysis result for this turn: intent, language, confidence.

extracted_opportunity.description: Best-effort structured extraction from user message.

matched_experts.description: Experts recommended for this opportunity across all matched units.

message_id.description: The ID of the assistant's message in the database. Needed by the FE.
message_id.title: Message Id

suggested_actions.description: Client-facing next actions like 'save opportunity draft', 'push to CRM', etc.

suggestions.description: Optional rich suggestion cards for FE (interactive UI).

user_message_id.description: The ID of the user's message in the database.

document_preview.description: First 200 chars of the embedded document.
document_preview.title: Document Preview

ConversationContext.description:
Output of the context analysis step (LLM call 0 in each chat turn).

Contains everything the router needs to decide which tool/flow to run.

clarification_needed.description: Follow-up question for the user when the message is too vague.
clarification_needed.title: Clarification Needed

opportunity_hint.description: Short summary of the opportunity described by the user, if any.
opportunity_hint.title: Opportunity Hint

ConversationContext.title: ConversationContext

ConversationCreate.description:
Request body to explicitly create a new conversation.

`first_message` is used to auto-generate a descriptive title via the
secondary LLM model. The conversation is created immediately; the title
is generated synchronously before the response is returned.

first_message.description: The user's opening message — used to generate the conversation title.
examples: We have a D365 CRM project for a retail chain in Japan.
first_message.title: First Message

ConversationCreate.title: ConversationCreate

ConversationMessagePublic.description: Single chat turn as returned by GET /chat/{conversation_id}.
ConversationMessagePublic.title: ConversationMessagePublic

ConversationSummary.description: Row in the conversation list (no messages).

last_message_preview.description: First 200 chars of the last user message for list preview.
last_message_preview.title: Last Message Preview

ConversationSummary.title: ConversationSummary

DevSmokeResult.description: Minimal health check for external APIs (no secrets in response).

detail.description: Short diagnostic (e.g. model name, counts). Never includes API keys.

post.description: Login or Register with Microsoft SSO

get.description: Returns conversations for the current user, most recently updated first.

schema.title: Response List Conversations Api V1 Chat Get

post.description: Creates a new conversation, processes `first_message` through the same intent-aware pipeline as POST /chat/{conversation_id}, persists messages, and generates a conversation title from the first message (secondary LLM). Returns `ChatResponse` with `conversation_id` for subsequent turns.
post.summary: Create conversation and send first message

get.description: Returns metadata and full message history (including assistant ui_payload). Owner only.

post.description: Intent-aware chat: same pipeline as POST /chat but for an existing `conversation_id` (path). Persists user + assistant turns and session context.
post.summary: Send a message in an existing conversation

post.description: Development-only bootstrap endpoint to create a superuser with email/password.
Runs the built-in unit seed flow (same as `python -m scripts.seed.units`). Upserts unit capability data into PostgreSQL and then re-indexes to Chroma. Disabled in production.

delete.description: Drops the entire unit_capabilities collection and recreates it empty. Run `python -m scripts.seed.units` again to repopulate. Disabled in production.

get.description: Returns every document currently indexed in the unit_capabilities collection. Disabled in production.

delete.description: Removes one unit by its Chroma ID. Disabled in production.
delete.summary: [DEV] Delete a single unit from ChromaDB

get.description: Calls the configured OpenRouter API to generate embeddings. Use to verify LLM_API_KEY. Disabled in production.
Calls HubSpot ``GET /settings/v3/users`` and returns user count. Use to verify HUBSPOT_API_KEY. Disabled in production.
Calls the configured OpenAI-compatible API with a one-token style prompt. Use to verify LLM_API_KEY and LLM_BASE_URL without going through chat. Disabled in production.
Calls the configured OpenRouter API with a bound tool to verify if function-calling works. Use `?model=...` to test a specific model. Disabled in production.
Simple liveness probe — returns 200 immediately with no dependency checks.

schema.title: Response Liveness Api V1 Health Get

get.description:
Readiness probe — checks all downstream services but never crashes the app.

Every check is wrapped in its own try/except so a missing service is
reported as an error in the response body rather than an unhandled
exception that would return a 5xx to the caller.

schema.title: Response Ready Api V1 Health Ready Get

get.description: Bundles the three metadata calls used to render the HubSpot deal form: pipelines, deal properties filtered to ``formField=true``, and users.
get.summary: Get pipelines + form-field properties + users (one round-trip)

post.description: Accepts the same JSON shape as the frontend ``DealDraft`` (camelCase keys). Maps fields to HubSpot property names and creates the deal via the HubSpot API.
post.summary: Create a HubSpot deal from form draft

post.description: Accepts a list of dictionaries. Returns a HubSpotDealCreateResponse.

schema.title: Response Get Deal Api V1 Hubspot Deals  Deal Id  Get

get.summary: Get a single HubSpot deal by ID

parameters.description: If true, return only properties with formField=true (deal form fields).
parameters.name: form_field_only

schema.description: If true, return only properties with formField=true (deal form fields).
schema.title: Form Field Only

get.summary: Get deal property metadata from HubSpot
Get HubSpot users (for owner / sub-owner fields)

get.description: US2: FE polls this endpoint; later can evolve to push/stream.

post.description: Creates a notification with flexible JSON payload for the opportunity-match-unit scenario.

get.description: US6: returns a consolidated list; currently local DB.

post.description: US5: persist an opportunity captured from chat or manual entry.

put.description: US5: requires explicit confirmation via request body.

schema.title: Response List Units Api V1 Units Get

get.summary: Get case studies for a unit
Get available staff and experts for a unit
